mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in nonfarm payroll employment indicate a robust labor market, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS rises for 3 consecutive months yet S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than half of such occurrences, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops below 0.5%, it signals extreme monetary accommodation that compresses discount rates and stimulates risk appetite, producing a sharp positive S&P 500 reaction within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS drops below 0.5%, the S&P 500 declines or fails to generate a median positive return over 5 trading days in the first two instances, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with a low federal funds rate creates a synergistic demand stimulus: consumers spend more due to confident outlook while cheap credit amplifies spending, leading to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint condition occurs but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly higher than when either condition alone is met (p<0.05), reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is above 6.5%, a month-over-month decline signals the labor market is healing, which reduces recession fears and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as the recovery gains traction.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE month-over-month decreases by at least 0.1 percentage point
failure_condition: If in the high unemployment regime (UNRATE > 6.5%), a monthly decline in UNRATE does not lead to a positive median 60-day S&P 500 return over 5 or more instances, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing activity and supply-side strength, which signals rising corporate output and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon of 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases over a 3-month period but the S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative, or if the rank correlation between INDPRO growth and 60-day returns is below 0.1 over a 5-year rolling window, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,500 thousand units signals a weakening housing market that depresses consumer wealth and construction employment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as economic fears mount.
failure_condition: If HOUST is below 1500 and the S&P 500 still produces a positive median 20-day return over at least 10 trigger instances, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls expand and retail sales simultaneously grow month-over-month, the joint signal of a strengthening labor market and consumer spending powerfully lifts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month increase > 0 and RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.2%
failure_condition: If the simultaneous increase condition is met but S&P 500 5-day return is negative in more than 40% of occurrences, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In an inflationary environment (core CPI year-over-year above 3%), a rising federal funds rate signals tightening monetary policy that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in a regime of CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%, a FEDFUNDS increase does not lead to a negative median S&P 500 20-day return over at least 5 tightening episodes, reject hypothesis.
